failed_attempts.drift_summary: Foundry's CTO interpreted the M1 brief as the entire project and re-decomposed into 6 modules covering M1-M6. Within Foundry's M1, the schemas/llm_client/tasks files were generic Celery scaffolding, not the clinical domain layer the brief specified. Chose Celery instead of brief-mandated ARQ; pulled in S3, encryption, PDF gen unrequested. Modified 22 baseline files the brief explicitly marked as fixed. Workspace preserved at ~/.shadow/workspace/proj_1777098587/ for analysis. Cancelled in DB.

Constraint-tightened request: short imperative passed as request, full brief copied as MODULE_BRIEF.md in workspace. Foundry's CTO obeyed planning-step constraints — clean 1-module / 3-phase / 6-sprint decomposition with correct file targets. But ForgePipeline's per-sprint code generation step ignored the 'do not modify baseline' rule and was already overwriting api/websocket.py, engines/rules/engine.py, migrations/env.py during sprint 1. Killed at 32:35 elapsed before sprint 1 finished. Workspace preserved at ~/.shadow/workspace/proj_1777110170/. Cancelled in DB.

notes: Initialized 2026-04-24. Session 1 will set project_id after first orch.plan() call.
2026-04-25: First Foundry attempt drifted hard from brief. Reset and re-launching with constraint-tightened request that bypasses CTO re-decomposition.

2026-04-25: Second Foundry attempt — CTO planning step obeyed constraints but ForgePipeline per-sprint generate step ignored read-only baseline rule. Killed early. Pivoting to hand-write M1 — brief is small enough (5 files / ~500 LOC) that hand-writing is cheaper than another Foundry round.
